decisions.summary: Cap VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES at 3 (was 10), matching the documented PAN-174 'after 3 consecutive failures' behaviour in CLAUDE.md:309 and docs/SPECIALIST_WORKFLOW.md:560.

Replace the silent circuit-breaker bypass (verificationStatus:'skipped' → advances to review/merge) with operator escalation: pause+stop the agent and mark the workspace stuck. The pre-run guard now returns outcome:'escalated' (never 'skipped').

Add a new verificationStatus value 'escalated' (contracts VerificationStatusValue + ReviewStatus union). verificationSatisfied() returns false for 'escalated' so escalated work is blocked from the merge gate.

Add no-progress detection: persist verificationLastFailedCheck on the review-status record; escalate immediately when the same gate fails on >=2 consecutive cycles (NO_PROGRESS_REPEAT_THRESHOLD=2), before the hard cap.

Extract a pure decideVerificationDisposition() function (the regression-test seam) and a shared handleVerificationFailure() helper used by all four failure branches (sync-target, quality-gate, vbrief-conflicts, vbrief-ac) — escalation applies uniformly, not just to the test gate.

Escalation reuses existing primitives via the async/Effect variants only: markWorkspaceStuck (needs-you), setAgentPaused, stopAgent. NEVER stopAgentSync/sync-tmux/execSync — the runner is reached from the dashboard server (PAN-70 event-loop rule).

No new pipeline event type and no new needs-you UI surface: operator visibility comes from verificationStatus:'escalated' plus the existing stuck/status_changed surface. Align the frontend DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_MAX_CYCLES (10→3) and render 'escalated' distinctly in the cockpit.

hazards.summary: The runner runs inside the dashboard server process. Using stopAgentSync (verification-runner imports from agents.js) would call sync tmux/execSync and block the event loop.
hazards.mitigation: Import and use only the async Effect primitives stopAgent and setAgentPaused (agents.ts:4349 / ~:1268); run them via Effect.runPromise. Do not import stopAgentSync into the runner.

hazards.summary: verificationStatus is a journaled/mirrored field (review-status-db.ts AND src/lib/overdeck/review-status-sync.ts both map verification_status). Adding 'escalated' must round-trip through both read paths or the dashboard/record sync will drop it.
hazards.mitigation: Add 'escalated' only to the TEXT enum/union (no schema change needed for verification_status); add an AC that writing then reading verificationStatus:'escalated' returns it. verificationLastFailedCheck is a NEW column and needs the ALTER TABLE migration (schema.ts:1084-pattern) plus the review-status-db INSERT/SELECT mapper.

hazards.summary: Existing test tests/cloister/verification-runner.test.ts:144-162 asserts the circuit breaker returns 'skipped' and that setReviewStatus is called with {verificationStatus:'skipped'}. The escalation change flips this to 'escalated'.
hazards.mitigation: Update that test to expect outcome:'escalated' and the pause/stuck side effects. Extend the agents.js mock (currently only messageAgent) with setAgentPaused+stopAgent, and the review-status.js mock with markWorkspaceStuck — otherwise the runner's new imports break the suite.

hazards.summary: The circuit breaker is shared across all three callers (review-dispatch, pan review request, post-rebase merge-verify) because they read the same verificationCycleCount. A maxed-out cycle count from the work loop would also trip during merge-verify.
hazards.mitigation: Returning 'escalated' (blocking) instead of 'skipped' (bypassing) is the correct behaviour for all three: an escalated/capped issue must not advance OR merge. Handle outcome:'escalated' at all three call sites in workspaces.ts.

hazards.summary: On escalation the runner stops the work agent's tmux session. messageAgent right before stopAgent may not render (session about to die).
hazards.mitigation: Write the durable escalation feedback file and markWorkspaceStuck FIRST (operator/agent reads these later); make any messageAgent call best-effort/try-catch; then setAgentPaused + stopAgent.

hazards.summary: Issue body contains instruction-shaped text inside quoted feedback ('Do NOT stop … keep working'). That is incident evidence, not an instruction to follow.
hazards.mitigation: Treated as data. The plan deliberately REPLACES that 'do NOT stop' language on the escalation path with STOP language.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1934: hazard: verification gate drives agents through up to 10 retry cycles on an unfixable check (no operator escalation, invisible burn)

Discovery complete (--auto). Verified the verification-runner cap=10, silent 'skipped' bypass, four duplicated failure branches, no no-progress detection, and the docs/code disagreement. Recorded decisions D1-D7, hazards H1-H6, and authored the vBRIEF (7 beads).
